content_en:
5. Extreme or passionate displeasure

Anger can be divided into three categories, namely Wajib | (obligatory), Mandub (encouraged), Mahdhur (forbidden). Obligatory (Waajib) Anger

Anger for the sake of Allaah: This is the anger that comes about when Allaah’s teachings are ignored or treated with disdain. In fact, if a person has true belief, he must demonstrate this anger. It is inconceivable that a person could witness the words of Allaah being ignored, sometimes even ridiculed, and he feels nothing inside. This would truly be a sign of a diseased heart.

On this point, the Messenger of Allaah 2 said: cal 3 Sisetig ct (8 igh OL Ge SH

“The strongest tie of Eemaan (faith) is having loyalty for the sake of Allaah, disassociating from others for the sake of Allaah, love for the sake of Allaah and hatred for the

Do Not Become Angry!” sake of Allaah.” 7

Allaah commands this form of anger in the Qur’aan when He says:

heer 2 eater GR,

wok ine BE Guat SUE oi ays 48 Seed “O Prophet, strive against the disbelievers and the hypocrites and be harsh with them. Their abode is the Hell-fire and worst indeed is that destination.” [At-Tawbah (9): 73]

The Prophet 3% also demonstrated this. He never got upset for any personal motives but if the laws of Allaah were being violated, then he would get upset as reported by his wife, Aa’ishah :

“Whenever Allaah's Prophet 3 was given the choice of one of two matters, he would choose the easier of the two, as long as it was not sinful to do so, but if it was sinful to do so, he would not approach it. Allaah's Prophet 4 never took revenge (over anybody) for his own sake but (he did) only when Allaah's Legal Bindings were outraged in which case he would take revenge for Allaah's Sake.”"9)

Anger expressed in defence of one’s right: From the Sharee’ah point of view, if a person’s rights are violated or he is wronged, he then has the right to get angry. He has the right to attempt to rectify that situation. Had this not been so, the earth would have

[22] Tayaalisi. [23] Al-Bukhaaree.

Golden Advice Series 03

been utterly corrupted by the spread of chaos and the destruction of the community.

Allaah says in the Qur’aan:

“and if Allaah did not check one set of people by means of another, the earth would indeed be full of mischief.” [AlBaqarah (2): 251]

That is because if a man does not get angry for his own self, he may be eliminated from this earth or made to be like animals which give servitude involuntarily and which do not get angry for their own sakes.”4! However, in expressing this anger, he does not have the right to do forbidden acts, such as cursing others or going to the extreme against them simply out of anger.

This is where one gets angry over the pettiest matters for which there is no call for a Muslim to get angry about. This type of anger goes beyond the degree of moderation, overwhelms the mind and religion, and rushes a person on the road of evil so unreasonably, that it may lead to one’s doom unknowingly. It may also lead one to commit the most grievous crimes and sins. Anger at this stage is blameworthy according to the Sharee’ah. This is the anger the Prophet # warned the one who sought his advice against and which forms the theme of this book.

[24] This condition is perhaps worse than that of the animals for we see that animals often charge at one who treats them cruelly however, flimsy and ineffective that protests may be.

| Causes & Effects of Anger

There are certain causes behind anger. The following are the most important of these causes:

Environment: The first cause behind anger is one’s surrounding; whether the close one, one’s family, or the far one, one’s community. One may be surrounded by evil people who consider rashness a sign of courage and anger that leads to injustice a sign of manhood. Thus, one gets this false impression and uncontrollable anger becomes a habit.

Disputation or arguing'*!: Competitors want to defeat one

[25] The Prophet 2 said:

another even if unfairly. When one of them cannot defeat the other, he gets angry or even infuriated, with the intention of tyrannizing over the other or taking revenge, especially if he sees himself stronger than his opponent.”

Joking falsely: If joking goes beyond the limits of truth, it will result in dispute. Dispute will incur anger deep down in the heart and in its turn will affect one’s emotional state and prompt him to tyranny and revenge. Perhaps that is why the Prophet 48 used to joke but only by using something true.

Showing enmity or aggression to others in any form: A man

“| guarantee a house in the surroundings of Paradise for a man who avoids quarrelling even if he were in the right, a house in the middle of Paradise for a man who avoids lying even if he were joking, and a house in the upper part of Paradise for a man who made his character good.” (Abu Dawood) [26] The Prophet 3 heard some people disputing about the Qur'aan. Thereupon he said:

ary BX dl OWS 1y55 Ng pS OW gs hile Lay salle Jy ols thes ty ctl Se ade ate

“It was because of this that those gone before you had perished. They set parts of the books against the others (whereas the fact is) that the Book of Allahhas | been revealed with one part confirming the others. Therefore, do not falsify | some parts with the others and speak only that which you know; that which you do not know, refer it to one who knows it well.” (At-Tirmidhee)

‘Do Not Become Angry!

gets angry and seeks taking revenge whenever he is exposed to any form of enmity or aggression such as mockery, derision, espionage, backbiting, calumniation, discrediting, imprisonment, beating, and torture. This aggression is shown most by un-Islamic and unjust authorities. That is perhaps why Allaah and His Prophet 3% warned against showing aggression to others without a legal requisite.

neg Mare oe ie he te WSS J we 8 2 7B WSS Yate oll Cp

Gi bie Bie Bs to G es eet yayl ay Stal eal oa CAME (Sh £35 Se Bh x aad,

<ipuist B Asis Aad “OQ you who believe! Let not a group scoff at another group, it may be that the latter are better than the former; nor let (some) women scoff at other women, it may be that the latter are better than the former, nor defame one another, nor insult one another by nicknames. How bad is it, to insult one’s brother after having faith. And whosoever does not repent, then such are indeed Zalimun (wrong-doers, etc.).” [Al-Hujuraat (49):11]

The Prophet ¢ said:

Bly V5 ctu 232 oa bg ABs mul pb VG AGE Vy UG V5 1255 Vy

Golden Advice Series 03 Be 8 “Br ooh dae a. sh

“ Avoid suspicion, for suspicion is the gravest lie in talk and do not spy upon one another, do not be inquisitive about one another, do not compete against one another, do not envy one another, do not hate one another, do not turn away from one another, and do not rupture one another, but be servants of Allaah and brethren. A Muslim is a brother of another Muslim, so he should not do injustice to him, nor should he hand him over (to an oppressor), nor should he forsake him... 7)

Unjustifiable haughtiness and pride: The person who is unjustifiably haughty and proud is adversely affected if he loses something that he thinks will keep his greatness and rank among people. If someone asks him to give him his right back, he gets infuriated. The same goes with being warned against committing a sin or being opposed in any matter. That haughty person believes that he is so perfect that none has the right to order, warn, or stop him. In reality, he is imperfect in all aspects and that is why he uses his haughtiness and pride to compensate for his inadequacies and imperfection.

Neglecting to strive against the part of the soul that incites evil: Any vice that is inflicted on man may grow and worsen and finally become second nature to him, because he does not take care to check it or etvell it up. That is by Allaah = calls to Sovin

[27] Al-Bukhaaree, Muslim (2564) Musnad Ahamad (7402).

“Do Not Become Angry! against one’s own desires.

hosel o al 35 bee he Mead ts L3g8 Ball

“And those who strive in Our (cause), We will certainly guide them to Our Paths: for verily Allaah is with those who do right.” [Al-Ankabut (29): 69]

Not supporting those who suffer from the flaw of anger: One may recognize his flaws and vices but because of his weakness before his desires, the temptations of devils among men and jinn, and the glitter of life he may be unable to get rid of these vices, At that time, others should stand by him in order to help to cure him of the flaw of anger. If left unchecked this flaw penetrates

| and worsens so that it becomes second nature to him and finally | he will be unable to get rid of it.

The Prophet 4 said: ‘ te’ orl Hetishelonnmesiasievees Ww Sy 3) Gyles sf Cw SET 52h “Help your brother whether he is an oppressor or an oppressed.”

A man said, “O Allaah’s Messenger! | will help him if he is oppressed, but if he is an oppressor, how shall | help him?” The Prophet 2% said:

“By preventing him from oppressing (others), for that is how to help him.”*!

Gaiden Advice Series 03 Labelling people with something that they think depreciates them: A man may hear someone describing him with something that he thinks attaches a deficiency or defect to him, such as ‘If you were really a man, you would surely face so and so, and | think that you are afraid of him’. Such sayings move him from inside and cause him anger and fury, which in turn push him to take revenge. To illustrate this, the following story of Umayyah bin Khalaf’s death in the Battle of Badr may be presented:

Abdullah bin Mas’ud 2 reported, “Sa’d bin Mu’az went on minor pilgrimage (Umrah) to Makkah. Sa’d used to stay at the house of Umayyah bin Khalaf Abu Safawan, and Umayyah himself used to stay at Sa’d’s house when he passed by Madeenah on his way to Ash-Sham (Syria). Umayyah asked Sa’d to wait till noon, when people at Makkah would be having a short rest, so that he could circumambulate the Ka’bah.
content_id:
5. Ketidaksenangan yang berlebihan atau penuh gairah

Kemarahan dapat dibagi menjadi tiga kategori, yaitu Wajib (kewajiban), Mandub (dianjurkan), Mahdhur (dilarang). Kemarahan yang Wajib (Wajib)

Marah karena Allah: Ini adalah kemarahan yang muncul ketika ajaran Allah diabaikan atau diperlakukan dengan hina. Bahkan, jika seseorang memiliki keimanan yang benar, ia harus menunjukkan kemarahan ini. Tidak masuk akal jika seseorang menyaksikan firman Allah diabaikan, bahkan terkadang diejek, namun ia tidak merasakan apa pun di dalam hatinya. Ini benar-benar merupakan tanda hati yang sakit.

Mengenai hal ini, Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: cal 3 Sisetig ct (8 igh OL Ge SH

"Ikatan iman yang paling kuat adalah setia karena Allah, berlepas diri dari orang lain karena Allah, mencintai karena Allah dan membenci karena

Jangan Marah!" Allah." 7

Allah memerintahkan bentuk kemarahan ini dalam Al-Qur'an ketika Dia berfirman:

heer 2 eater GR,

wok ine BE Guat SUE oi ays 48 Seed "Wahai Nabi, berjihadlah melawan orang-orang kafir dan orang-orang munafik serta bersikap keraslah terhadap mereka. Tempat mereka adalah neraka Jahannam dan itulah seburuk-buruk tempat kembali." [At-Tawbah (9): 73]

Nabi ﷺ juga menunjukkan hal ini. Beliau tidak pernah marah karena motif pribadi, tetapi jika hukum-hukum Allah dilanggar, maka beliau akan marah sebagaimana diriwayatkan oleh istrinya, Aisyah :

"Kapan pun Rasulullah ﷺ diberi pilihan antara dua perkara, beliau akan memilih yang lebih mudah dari keduanya, selama hal itu tidak berdosa. Namun jika hal itu berdosa, beliau tidak akan mendekatinya. Rasulullah ﷺ tidak pernah membalas dendam (terhadap siapa pun) untuk kepentingan pribadinya, tetapi beliau hanya melakukannya ketika batasan-batasan hukum Allah dilanggar, dalam hal ini beliau akan membalas demi Allah."(9)

Kemarahan yang diekspresikan untuk membela hak seseorang: Dari sudut pandang syariat, jika hak seseorang dilanggar atau ia dizalimi, maka ia berhak untuk marah. Ia berhak untuk berusaha memperbaiki keadaan tersebut. Seandainya tidak demikian, niscaya bumi akan

[22] Tayaalisi. [23] Al-Bukhaaree.

Golden Advice Series 03

benar-benar rusak oleh meluasnya kekacauan dan kehancuran masyarakat.

Allah berfirman dalam Al-Qur'an:

"Dan seandainya Allah tidak menolak (keganasan) sebagian manusia dengan sebagian yang lain, niscaya bumi ini benar-benar penuh dengan kerusakan." [Al-Baqarah (2): 251]

Hal itu karena jika seseorang tidak marah untuk dirinya sendiri, ia mungkin akan dilenyapkan dari muka bumi ini atau dijadikan seperti binatang yang memberikan pengabdian secara tidak sukarela dan yang tidak marah untuk kepentingan diri mereka sendiri."4! Namun, dalam mengekspresikan kemarahan ini, ia tidak berhak melakukan perbuatan yang diharamkan, seperti mengutuk orang lain atau bersikap berlebihan terhadap mereka hanya karena marah.

Di sinilah seseorang marah atas perkara-perkara yang paling sepele yang tidak sepatutnya seorang Muslim marah karenanya. Jenis kemarahan ini melampaui batas kewajaran, mengalahkan akal dan agama, serta menyeret seseorang ke jalan kejahatan secara tidak masuk akal, sehingga dapat mengantarkannya kepada kebinasaan tanpa disadari. Hal ini juga dapat menjerumuskannya untuk melakukan kejahatan dan dosa yang paling berat. Kemarahan pada tahap ini tercela menurut syariat. Inilah kemarahan yang Nabi ﷺ peringatkan kepada orang yang meminta nasihat beliau dan yang menjadi tema buku ini.

[24] Keadaan ini mungkin lebih buruk daripada keadaan binatang, karena kita melihat bahwa binatang sering menyerang siapa pun yang memperlakukan mereka dengan kejam, betapapun lemah dan tidak efektifnya protes tersebut.

| Sebab-sebab & Dampak Kemarahan

Ada beberapa sebab di balik kemarahan. Berikut ini adalah sebab-sebab yang paling penting:

Lingkungan: Sebab pertama di balik kemarahan adalah lingkungan seseorang; baik yang dekat, yaitu keluarganya, maupun yang jauh, yaitu masyarakatnya. Seseorang mungkin dikelilingi oleh orang-orang jahat yang menganggap kecerobohan sebagai tanda keberanian dan kemarahan yang berujung pada kezaliman sebagai tanda kejantanan. Dengan demikian, seseorang mendapatkan kesan yang salah ini dan kemarahan yang tidak terkendali menjadi kebiasaan.

Pertengkaran atau perdebatan'*!: Para pesaing ingin mengalahkan satu

[25] Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

sama lain meskipun dengan cara yang tidak adil. Ketika salah satu dari mereka tidak dapat mengalahkan yang lain, ia menjadi marah atau bahkan murka, dengan niat untuk menindas yang lain atau membalas dendam, terutama jika ia melihat dirinya lebih kuat dari lawannya."

Bercanda dengan dusta: Jika candaan melampaui batas kebenaran, maka akan mengakibatkan perselisihan. Perselisihan akan menimbulkan kemarahan yang mendalam di dalam hati dan pada gilirannya akan memengaruhi keadaan emosional seseorang serta mendorongnya kepada kezaliman dan balas dendam. Mungkin itulah sebabnya Nabi ﷺ biasa bercanda tetapi hanya dengan sesuatu yang benar.

Menunjukkan permusuhan atau agresi terhadap orang lain dalam bentuk apa pun: Seseorang

"Aku menjamin sebuah rumah di pinggiran surga bagi orang yang meninggalkan perdebatan meskipun ia benar, sebuah rumah di tengah surga bagi orang yang meninggalkan dusta meskipun ia bercanda, dan sebuah rumah di bagian atas surga bagi orang yang membaguskan akhlaknya." (Abu Dawood) [26] Nabi ﷺ mendengar beberapa orang berdebat tentang Al-Qur'an. Maka beliau bersabda:

ary BX dl OWS 1y55 Ng pS OW gs hile Lay salle Jy ols thes ty ctl Se ade ate

"Karena inilah orang-orang sebelum kalian binasa. Mereka mempertentangkan sebagian Kitab dengan sebagian yang lain (padahal faktanya) bahwa Kitabullah diturunkan dengan sebagian membenarkan sebagian yang lain. Oleh karena itu, janganlah kalian mendustakan sebagian dengan sebagian yang lain dan bicaralah hanya tentang apa yang kalian ketahui; yang tidak kalian ketahui, serahkanlah kepada orang yang mengetahuinya dengan baik." (At-Tirmidhee)

Jangan Marah!

menjadi marah dan berusaha membalas dendam setiap kali ia menghadapi segala bentuk permusuhan atau agresi seperti ejekan, cemoohan, memata-matai, ghibah, fitnah, pencemaran nama baik, pemenjaraan, pemukulan, dan penyiksaan. Agresi ini paling banyak ditunjukkan oleh pemerintahan yang tidak Islami dan zalim. Mungkin itulah sebabnya Allah dan Rasul-Nya ﷺ memperingatkan agar tidak menunjukkan agresi terhadap orang lain tanpa dasar hukum yang sah.

neg Mare oe ie he te WSS J we 8 2 7B WSS Yate oll Cp

Gi bie Bie Bs to G es eet yayl ay Stal eal oa CAME (Sh £35 Se Bh x aad,

<ipuist B Asis Aad "Wahai orang-orang yang beriman! Janganlah suatu kaum mengolok-olok kaum yang lain, karena boleh jadi mereka (yang diolok-olok) lebih baik dari mereka (yang mengolok-olok); dan jangan pula perempuan-perempuan (mengolok-olok) perempuan lain, karena boleh jadi mereka (yang diolok-olok) lebih baik dari mereka (yang mengolok-olok); dan janganlah kalian saling mencela satu sama lain, dan janganlah kalian saling memanggil dengan gelar-gelar yang buruk. Seburuk-buruk panggilan adalah (panggilan) yang buruk sesudah beriman. Dan barangsiapa tidak bertaubat, maka mereka itulah orang-orang yang zalim." [Al-Hujuraat (49):11]

Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

Bly V5 ctu 232 oa bg ABs mul pb VG AGE Vy UG V5 1255 Vy

Golden Advice Series 03 Be 8 "Br ooh dae a. sh

"Jauhilah prasangka, karena prasangka adalah sedusta-dusta ucapan. Janganlah kalian saling memata-matai, janganlah saling mencari-cari kesalahan, janganlah saling bersaing secara tidak sehat, janganlah saling hasad, janganlah saling membenci, janganlah saling membelakangi, dan janganlah saling memutuskan hubungan. Jadilah hamba-hamba Allah yang bersaudara. Seorang Muslim adalah saudara Muslim lainnya, maka ia tidak boleh menzaliminya, tidak boleh menyerahkannya (kepada orang yang zalim), dan tidak boleh mengabaikannya... 7)

Kesombongan dan keangkuhan yang tidak dapat dibenarkan: Orang yang sombong dan angkuh secara tidak dapat dibenarkan akan terpengaruh buruk jika ia kehilangan sesuatu yang menurutnya akan menjaga keagungan dan kedudukannya di antara manusia. Jika seseorang memintanya untuk mengembalikan haknya, ia menjadi murka. Begitu pula halnya jika diperingatkan agar tidak melakukan dosa atau ditentang dalam suatu perkara. Orang yang sombong itu percaya bahwa dirinya begitu sempurna sehingga tidak seorang pun berhak memerintah, memperingatkan, atau melarangnya. Padahal kenyataannya, ia tidak sempurna dalam segala aspek dan itulah sebabnya ia menggunakan kesombongan dan keangkuhannya untuk menutupi kekurangan dan ketidaksempurnaannya.

Lalai berjuang melawan bagian jiwa yang menghasut kepada kejahatan: Setiap keburukan yang menimpa manusia dapat tumbuh dan memburuk hingga akhirnya menjadi tabiat baginya, karena ia tidak berusaha untuk mengendalikan atau menghilangkannya. Itulah sebabnya Allah menyeru untuk berjuang

[27] Al-Bukhaaree, Muslim (2564) Musnad Ahamad (7402).

Jangan Marah! melawan hawa nafsu sendiri.

hosel o al 35 bee he Mead ts L3g8 Ball

"Dan orang-orang yang berjihad di (jalan) Kami, sungguh akan Kami tunjukkan kepada mereka jalan-jalan Kami: sesungguhnya Allah benar-benar beserta orang-orang yang berbuat kebaikan." [Al-Ankabut (29): 69]

Tidak mendukung mereka yang menderita cacat kemarahan: Seseorang mungkin menyadari kekurangan dan keburukannya, tetapi karena kelemahannya di hadapan hawa nafsunya, godaan setan dari kalangan manusia dan jin, serta gemerlapnya kehidupan dunia, ia mungkin tidak mampu menyingkirkan keburukan-keburukan ini. Pada saat itu, orang lain harus berdiri di sampingnya untuk membantunya menyembuhkan cacat kemarahannya. Jika dibiarkan, cacat ini akan merasuk

| dan memburuk sehingga menjadi tabiat baginya dan akhirnya | ia tidak akan mampu menyingkirkannya.

Nabi ﷺ bersabda: ' te' orl Hetishelonnmesiasievees Ww Sy 3) Gyles sf Cw SET 52h "Tolonglah saudaramu baik ia sebagai orang yang zalim maupun yang dizalimi."

Seorang laki-laki berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah! Aku akan menolongnya jika ia dizalimi, tetapi jika ia sebagai orang yang zalim, bagaimana aku menolongnya?" Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Dengan mencegahnya dari berbuat zalim (terhadap orang lain), karena demikianlah cara menolongnya."*!

Golden Advice Series 03 Melabeli orang dengan sesuatu yang menurut mereka merendahkan mereka: Seseorang mungkin mendengar orang lain menggambarkannya dengan sesuatu yang menurutnya melekatkan kekurangan atau cacat padanya, seperti 'Kalau kamu benar-benar laki-laki, pasti kamu akan menghadapi si fulan, dan aku rasa kamu takut padanya'. Ucapan-ucapan seperti itu menggerakkannya dari dalam dan menimbulkan kemarahan serta kemurkaan, yang pada gilirannya mendorongnya untuk membalas dendam. Untuk mengilustrasikan hal ini, kisah berikut tentang kematian Umayyah bin Khalaf dalam Perang Badr dapat disajikan:

Abdullah bin Mas'ud meriwayatkan, "Sa'd bin Mu'az pergi melaksanakan ibadah umrah ke Makkah. Sa'd biasa menginap di rumah Umayyah bin Khalaf Abu Safawan, dan Umayyah sendiri biasa menginap di rumah Sa'd ketika ia melewati Madinah dalam perjalanannya ke Ash-Sham (Syiria). Umayyah meminta Sa'd untuk menunggu hingga tengah hari, ketika orang-orang di Makkah sedang beristirahat sejenak, agar ia dapat melakukan tawaf mengelilingi Ka'bah.
content_ar: 5. Extreme or passionate displeasure الغضب can be divided into three categories, أي الواجب | (obligatory), Mandub (encouraged), Mahdhur (forbidden). Obligatory (Waajib) الغضب الغضب for the sake of Allaah: This is the الغضب that comes about عندما Allaah’s teachings are ignored or treated with disdain. In fact, إذا a person has true الاعتقاد, he must demonstrate this الغضب. It is inconceivable that a person could witness the words of Allaah being ignored, sometimes even ridiculed, and he feels nothing inside. This would truly be a sign of a diseased القلب. On this point, الرسول ﷺ of Allaah 2 said: cal 3 Sisetig ct (8 igh OL Ge SH “The strongest tie of Eemaan (الإيمان) is having loyalty for the sake of Allaah, disassociating from others for the sake of Allaah, المحبة for the sake of Allaah and hatred for the Do Not Become Angry!” sake of Allaah.” 7 Allaah commands this form of الغضب in the Qur’aan عندما He says: heer 2 eater GR, wok ine BE Guat SUE oi ays 48 Seed “O النبي, strive against the disbelievers and the hypocrites and be harsh with them. Their abode is the النار-fire and worst indeed is that destination.” [At-التوبة (9): 73] النبي ﷺ 3% أيضاً demonstrated this. He never got upset for any personal motives but إذا the laws of Allaah were being violated, then he would get upset as reported by his الزوجة, Aa’ishah : “كلما Allaah's النبي 3 was given the choice of one of two matters, he would choose the easier of the two, as long as it was not sinful to do لذا, but إذا it was sinful to do لذا, he would not approach it. Allaah's النبي 4 never took revenge (over anybody) for his own sake but (he did) only عندما Allaah's Legal Bindings were outraged in which case he would take revenge for Allaah's Sake.”"9) الغضب expressed in defence of one’s الحق: From the Sharee’ah point of view, إذا a person’s الحقوق are violated or he is wronged, he then has the الحق to get angry. He has the الحق to attempt to rectify that situation. Had this not been لذا, the earth would have [22] Tayaalisi. [23] البخاري. Golden Advice Series 03 been utterly corrupted by the spread of chaos and the destruction of the الجماعة. Allaah says in the Qur’aan: “and إذا Allaah did not check one set of people by means of another, the earth would indeed be full of mischief.” [AlBaqarah (2): 251] That is لأن إذا a man does not get angry for his own self, he may be eliminated from this earth or made to be like animals which give servitude involuntarily and which do not get angry for their own sakes.”4! غير أن, in expressing this الغضب, he does not have the الحق to do forbidden acts, مثل cursing others or going to the extreme against them simply out of الغضب. This is where one gets angry over the pettiest matters for which there is no call for a مسلم to get angry about. This type of الغضب goes beyond the degree of moderation, overwhelms the العقل and religion, and rushes a person on the road of الشر لذا unreasonably, that it may lead to one’s doom unknowingly. It may أيضاً lead one to commit the most grievous crimes and الذنوب. الغضب at this stage is blameworthy وفقاً لـ the Sharee’ah. This is the الغضب النبي ﷺ # warned the one who sought his advice against and which forms the theme of this الكتاب. [24] This condition is perhaps worse than that of the animals for we see that animals often charge at one who treats them cruelly غير أن, flimsy and ineffective that protests may be. | Causes & Effects of الغضب There are certain causes behind الغضب. The following are the most المهم of these causes: Environment: The أولاً cause behind الغضب is one’s surrounding; whether the close one, one’s الأسرة, or the far one, one’s الجماعة. One may be surrounded by الشر people who consider rashness a sign of الشجاعة and الغضب that leads to الظلم a sign of manhood. وهكذا, one gets this false impression and uncontrollable الغضب becomes a habit. Disputation or arguing'*!: Competitors want to defeat one [25] النبي ﷺ 2 said: another even إذا unfairly. عندما one of them cannot defeat the other, he gets angry or even infuriated, with the intention of tyrannizing over the other or taking revenge, ولا سيما إذا he sees himself stronger than his opponent.” Joking falsely: إذا joking goes beyond the limits of الحق, it will result in dispute. Dispute will incur الغضب deep down in the القلب and in its turn will affect one’s emotional state and prompt him to tyranny and revenge. Perhaps that is why النبي ﷺ 48 used to joke but only by using something true. Showing enmity or aggression to others in any form: A man “| guarantee a house in the surroundings of الجنة for a man who avoids quarrelling even إذا he were in the الحق, a house in the middle of الجنة for a man who avoids lying even إذا he were joking, and a house in the upper part of الجنة for a man who made his الأخلاق الخير.” (أبو داود) [26] النبي ﷺ 3 heard some people disputing about the Qur'aan. Thereupon قال: ary BX dl OWS 1y55 Ng pS OW gs hile Lay salle Jy ols thes ty ctl Se ade ate “It was لأن of this that those gone قبل you had perished. They set parts of the الكتب against the others (في حين the fact is) that the الكتاب of Allahhas | been revealed with one part confirming the others. لذلك, do not falsify | some parts with the others and speak only that which you know; that which you do not know, refer it to one who knows it well.” (At-Tirmidhee) ‘Do Not Become Angry! gets angry and seeks taking revenge كلما he is exposed to any form of enmity or aggression مثل mockery, derision, espionage, backbiting, calumniation, discrediting, imprisonment, beating, and torture. This aggression is shown most by un-Islamic and unjust authorities. That is perhaps why Allaah and His النبي 3% warned against showing aggression to others without a legal requisite. neg Mare oe ie he te WSS J we 8 2 7B WSS Yate oll Cp Gi bie Bie Bs to G es eet yayl ay Stal eal oa CAME (Sh £35 Se Bh x aad, <ipuist B Asis Aad “OQ you who believe! Let not a group scoff at another group, it may be that the latter are better than the former; nor let (some) women scoff at other women, it may be that the latter are better than the former, nor defame بعضهم بعضاً, nor insult بعضهم بعضاً by nicknames. How bad is it, to insult one’s الأخ بعد having الإيمان. And whosoever does not repent, then such are indeed Zalimun (الخطأ-doers, etc.).” [Al-Hujuraat (49):11] النبي ﷺ ¢ said: Bly V5 ctu 232 oa bg ABs mul pb VG AGE Vy UG V5 1255 Vy Golden Advice Series 03 Be 8 “Br ooh dae a. sh “ Avoid suspicion, for suspicion is the gravest lie in talk and do not spy upon بعضهم بعضاً, do not be inquisitive about بعضهم بعضاً, do not compete against بعضهم بعضاً, do not الحسد بعضهم بعضاً, do not الكراهية بعضهم بعضاً, do not turn away from بعضهم بعضاً, and do not rupture بعضهم بعضاً, but be servants of Allaah and brethren. A مسلم is a الأخ of another مسلم, لذا he should not do الظلم to him, nor should he hand him over (to an oppressor), nor should he forsake him... 7) Unjustifiable haughtiness and الكبر: The person who is unjustifiably haughty and proud is adversely affected إذا he loses something that he thinks will keep his greatness and rank among people. إذا someone asks him to give him his الحق back, he gets infuriated. The same goes with being warned against committing a الذنب or being opposed in any matter. That haughty person believes that he is لذا perfect that none has the الحق to order, warn, or stop him. In reality, he is imperfect in all aspects and that is why he uses his haughtiness and الكبر to compensate for his inadequacies and imperfection. Neglecting to strive against the part of the النفس that incites الشر: Any vice that is inflicted on man may grow and worsen and وأخيراً become ثانياً nature to him, لأن he does not take care to check it or etvell it up. That is by Allaah = calls to Sovin [27] البخاري, مسلم (2564) Musnad Ahamad (7402). “Do Not Become Angry! against one’s own desires. hosel o al 35 bee he Mead ts L3g8 Ball “And those who strive in Our (cause), We will certainly guide them to Our Paths: for verily Allaah is with those who do الحق.” [Al-Ankabut (29): 69] Not supporting those who suffer from the flaw of الغضب: One may recognize his flaws and vices but لأن of his الضعف قبل his desires, the temptations of devils among men and الجن, and the glitter of الحياة he may be unable to get rid of these vices, At that time, others should stand by him من أجل help to الدواء him of the flaw of الغضب. إذا left unchecked this flaw penetrates | and worsens لذا that it becomes ثانياً nature to him and وأخيراً | he will be unable to get rid of it. النبي ﷺ 4 said: ‘ te’ orl Hetishelonnmesiasievees Ww Sy 3) Gyles sf Cw SET 52h “Help your الأخ whether he is an oppressor or an oppressed.” A man said, “O Allaah’s Messenger! | will help him إذا he is oppressed, but إذا he is an oppressor, how shall | help him?” النبي ﷺ 2% said: “By preventing him from oppressing (others), for that is how to help him.”*! Gaiden Advice Series 03 Labelling people with something that they think depreciates them: A man may hear someone describing him with something that he thinks attaches a deficiency or defect to him, مثل ‘إذا you were really a man, you would surely face لذا and لذا, and | think that you are afraid of him’. Such sayings move him from inside and cause him الغضب and fury, which in turn push him to take revenge. To illustrate this, the following story of Umayyah bin Khalaf’s الموت in the Battle of Badr may be presented: Abdullah bin Mas’ud 2 reported, “Sa’d bin Mu’az went on minor pilgrimage (العمرة) to مكة المكرمة. Sa’d used to stay at the house of Umayyah bin Khalaf Abu Safawan, and Umayyah himself used to stay at Sa’d’s house عندما he passed by Madeenah on his الطريق to Ash-Sham (Syria). Umayyah asked Sa’d to wait till noon, عندما people at مكة المكرمة would be having a short rest, لذا that he could circumambulate the Ka’bah.
